title: Bat Removal & Exclusion in White Plains, NY | $900 – $2,500
metaDescription: Professional bat removal and exclusion services in White Plains, Westchester. Humane, effective solutions for homes and businesses. Licensed experts. Call today!
intro: White Plains residents facing bat infestations need swift, humane, and permanent solutions. Our expert bat removal and exclusion services protect your home or business from these nocturnal invaders while ensuring compliance with wildlife protection laws. Serving all of White Plains and greater Westchester County, we provide thorough inspections, safe bat removal, and complete exclusion work to keep bats out for good.
whyUs: Best Pest Control NYC brings over a decade of specialized bat removal experience to White Plains, combining humane wildlife practices with cutting-edge exclusion technology. Our licensed technicians understand the unique architecture of White Plains homes—from historic properties near downtown to modern residences in Battle Hill—and tailor solutions to your specific property needs.
localContext: White Plains' mix of older Tudor and Colonial homes, along with multi-family buildings near Mamaroneck Avenue and the downtown corridor, often feature the attic spaces, chimneys, and eave gaps that attract roosting bats. The city's tree-lined neighborhoods and proximity to parks like Silver Lake and Saxon Woods create ideal habitats where bats thrive, making professional exclusion essential for property owners.

faqs.question: How much does bat removal cost in White Plains?
faqs.answer: Bat removal and exclusion services in White Plains typically range from $900 to $2,500, depending on the size of the colony, number of entry points, and the complexity of your property's structure. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: Is bat removal legal in White Plains, NY?
faqs.answer: Yes, but bat removal must follow New York State wildlife regulations. Bats are protected species, and removal cannot occur during maternity season (roughly May through August) when flightless pups are present. Our licensed professionals ensure all work complies with state and local laws while humanely excluding bats from your property.

faqs.question: What are signs of bats in my White Plains home?
faqs.answer: Common signs include squeaking or scratching sounds at dusk or dawn, dark stains near entry points, accumulation of droppings (guano) in attics or near walls, and sightings of bats entering or exiting your home at twilight. A strong ammonia-like odor from guano accumulation is also a telltale sign of a bat colony.

faqs.question: How long does bat exclusion take in White Plains?
faqs.answer: Most bat exclusion projects in White Plains are completed within 1-3 days for the initial installation of one-way exclusion devices and sealing of entry points. Devices typically remain in place for 3-7 days to ensure all bats have exited. We then return to remove devices, seal final openings, and perform cleanup as needed.
